channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Catholic priest and Indigenous activist Pérez killed by 2 gunmen in southern Mexico
item.description: TAPACHULA, Mexico (AP) — Catholic priest Marcelo Pérez, an activist for Indigenous peoples and farm laborers in southern Mexico, was killed on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Expelled Indian high commissioner denies involvement in murder of Sikh leader in Canada
item.description: VANCOUVER, British Columbia (AP) — India’s high commissioner to Canada has denied any involvement in the murder of a Canadian Sikh leader who was killed in British Columbia last year even though the Canadian government has named him as a person of interest in the assassination.
item.dc:creator: JIM MORRIS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: German authorities arrest Libyan man suspected of planning attack on Israeli Embassy
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — German authorities said Sunday that they have arrested a Libyan national with suspected ties to the extremist Islamic State group who was allegedly planning a firearms attack on the Israeli Embassy.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Sanders: Trump will be 'closer to Netanyahu' on Gaza if reelected
item.description: Sen. Bernie Sanders (I-Vt.) said Sunday he believes former President Trump will have similar views of the Israel-Hamas war to those of Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu if he is reelected.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Cuba gets some electricity back after major power outage left millions in the dark
item.description: HAVANA (AP) — Some electricity was restored in Cuba, the government said Saturday, after the island nation's worst blackout in at least two years left millions without electricity for two days.
item.dc:creator: ANDREA RODRIGUEZ, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Children greet King Charles III and Queen Camilla outside a Sydney church
item.description: SYDNEY (AP) — King Charles III and Queen Camilla were greeted by children Sunday outside a Sydney church in their first public appearance of their Australian visit.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Guatemalan judge grants investigative journalist Zamora house arrest and his family celebrates
item.description: GUATEMALA CITY (AP) — The family of a Guatemalan journalist on Saturday celebrated his transfer to house arrest following his jailing for more than two years amid his daily newspaper's anti-corruption investigations.
item.dc:creator: SONIA PEREZ D., Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ukraine’s ‘victory plan’ receives mixed reactions from Western allies
item.description: President Volodymyr Zelenskyy’s plan to end Ukraine’s nearly three-year war with Russia is getting mixed reactions from Western allies.
item.dc:creator: Hanna Arhirova, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Video published by Ukraine allegedly shows North Korean soldiers in Russia
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — A video purporting to show dozens of North Korean recruits lining up to collect Russian military fatigues has been released by Ukrainian officials, who say it shows the introduction of troops sent by Pyongyang into the conflict.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: France vows support for Ukraine's plan to end Russian invasion
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — France's foreign minister pledged his support for Ukraine’s plan for ending the war with Russia, telling reporters in Kyiv on Saturday that he would work with Ukrainian officials to secure other nations’ backing for the proposal.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Funeral home in Poland apologizes after a corpse falls out of a hearse in traffic
item.description: WARSAW, Poland (AP) — A funeral home in Poland issued an unusual apology on Saturday after a corpse that it was transporting fell out of a hearse and into traffic.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Hezbollah fires rocket salvo at northern Israel, Iranian leader vows 'Axis of Resistance' will continue
item.description: The Israel Defense Forces said at least 180 projectiles were fired into Israel from Lebanon as Hezbollah ramps up its attacks on the Jewish state with backing from Iran.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: UAV launched toward Israeli PM Netanyahu's private residence in Caesarea
item.description: A UAV was launched towards Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's private residence in the city of Caesarea on Saturday, but he and his wife were not home when it happened.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Belgrade's landmark Hotel Yugoslavia faces likely demolition, with many opposed
item.description: BELGRADE, Serbia (AP) — Belgrade’s iconic Hotel Yugoslavia, once a symbol of progress in the former socialist state of Yugoslavia that broke apart in the 1990s and a favorite gathering place for local residents as well as world leaders, now stands in eerie silence awaiting its likely demolition.
item.dc:creator: DUSAN STOJANOVIC, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Middle East latest: Hezbollah says its war with Israel is entering a new phase
item.description: Lebanon’s Hezbollah militant group said Friday it is entering a new phase in its fight against invading Israeli troops, as the region reckons with the killing of top Hamas leader Yahya Sinwar in a battle with Israeli forces in Gaza. Hamas acknowledged Sinwar’s death and described him as a martyr.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Cuba’s grid goes offline with massive blackout after a major power plant fails
item.description: HAVANA (AP) — Cuba’s electrical grid went down Friday after one of the island’s major power plants failed, a day after a massive blackout swept across the Caribbean island and with no official estimate for when service will be restored.
item.dc:creator: ANDREA RODRÍGUEZ, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: North Korea troops readying for Ukraine combat: South Korea
item.description: South Korea said it's used facial recognition AI tech to identify North Korean officers in eastern Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Ju-min Park, NewsNation

item.title: Two Venezuelan men shot in head in Juarez
item.description: Police in Juarez, Mexico, are investigating a shooting that left one Venezuelan dead, and another critically injured on Wednesday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, NewsNation

item.title: IDF video: Israeli tank fires at building where Hamas leader Yahya Sinwar was barricaded before being killed
item.description: A building where Hamas chief Yahya Sinwar had been barricaded was targeted by Israeli tank fire before the terror leader was killed on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Gangs attack neighborhoods across Haiti's capital in new wave of violence
item.description: PORT-AU-PRINCE, Haiti (AP) — Multiple gangs attacked several communities in the Haitian capital of Port-au-Prince on Friday, setting fire to homes and a church as bullets whizzed through the area. At least one woman was killed as gangs opened fire in Solino, St.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Sinwar's ruthless brother Mohammed slated to take over as leader of Hamas
item.description: Mohammed Sinwar is expected to take over as leader of Hamas after his older brother Yahya Sinwar was confirmed to have been killed in Gaza by the Israel Defense Forces as the brutal war continues.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Whelan says Brittney Griner was 'great help' in advocating for his freedom
item.description: Former U.S. Marine Paul Whelan said WNBA star Brittney Griner was of “great help” in advocating for his freedom after being detained in Russia since 2018.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Ex-Indian government employee charged in US assassination plot
item.description: The Justice Department announced charges against a former Indian government employee in the murder plot against dual U.S.-Canadian citizen Gurpatwant Singh Pannun, a prominent Sikh separatist.
item.dc:creator: Sarakshi Rai, The Hill

item.title: Video captures water tank falling on woman — but she miraculously escapes injury
item.description: A woman has escaped injury in Surat, India, after a water tank fell from a roof and landed perfectly around her, a viral video shows.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How the Uluburun shipwreck was discovered accidentally by a sponge diver
item.description: The Uluburun shipwreck was accidently discovered by a sponge diver in 1982. Following the discovery, excavation of the area took archaeologists a decade to complete.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hezbollah vows a new phase in the war, as Israel says it has killed Hamas leader Sinwar
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — The Lebanese militant group Hezbollah vowed to launch a new phase of fighting against invading Israeli troops Friday, a day after Israel said its forces in the Palestinian enclave of Gaza had killed Hamas leader Yahya Sinwar, the chief architect of last year’s brutal attack on Israe
item.dc:creator: JULIA FRANKEL, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Sinwar's removal from battlefield is ‘significant,’ presents opportunity for cease-fire, State Department says
item.description: The State Department on Thursday said the killing of Hamas leader Yahya Sinwar presents an "opportunity" for a potential cease-fire and an end to the yearlong war.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel says Hamas' top leader Yahya Sinwar was killed in Gaza
item.description: Israel’s foreign minister has confirmed that Israeli troops in Gaza have killed Hamas’ top leader Yahya Sinwar, a chief architect of last year's attack on Israel that sparked the war.
item.dc:creator: Wafaa Shurafa, Samy Magdy, Jack Jeffery, WGN 9

item.title: Roman-era graves found by homeowner during basement renovation project
item.description: During the winter of 2023-2024, archaeologists were called to a home in the suburbs of Paris after a homeowner discovered a skeleton during basement renovations.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hamas terror leader Yahya Sinwar killed in Gaza, Israel says
item.description: Israeli officials announced Thursday that Hamas leader Yahya Sinwar was killed during military operations inside the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: North Korea says its revised constitution defines South Korea as 'hostile state' for first time
item.description: South Korea condemned the North's constitutional revision as “an anti-unification, anti-national act.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Biden to travel to Germany Thursday, delays African leg of trip
item.description: In Berlin, Biden will convene a meeting with German Chancellor Olaf Scholz to discuss continued support for Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Tanya Noury, NewsNation

item.title: An Australian police sergeant likely to be charged over a Nazi salute
item.description: An Australian state police chief apologized to the Jewish community after a sergeant allegedly performed a Nazi salute.
item.dc:creator: Rod McGuirk, The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: China deploys record 125 warplanes in large-scale military drill in warning to Taiwan
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — China employed a record 125 aircraft, as well as its Liaoning aircraft carrier and ships, in large-scale military exercises surrounding Taiwan and its outlying islands Monday, simulating the sealing off of key ports in a move that underscores the tense situation in the Taiwan S
item.dc:creator: JOHNSON LAI and HUIZHONG WU, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Vial linked to UK woman's death had enough poison to kill thousands
item.description: Sturgess and her partner collapsed after they came into contact with a discarded perfume bottle containing the nerve agent.
item.dc:creator: DANICA KIRKA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israel says 4 soldiers killed by Hezbollah drone attack while Israeli strike in Gaza leaves 20 dead
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — A Hezbollah drone attack on an army base in central Israel killed four soldiers and severely wounded seven others Sunday, the military said, in the deadliest strike by the militant group since Israel launched its ground invasion of Lebanon nearly two weeks ago.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, NewsNation
